steps.title: Welcome to Day 2
steps.body: Yesterday you learned how to ask AI a clear question. Today you learn a trick that doubles the quality of every answer: give AI a role to play.

steps.title: What is a role prompt?
steps.body: A role prompt tells AI who to be before you ask your question. Instead of just asking, you say: "Act as a friendly editor" or "You are an expert planner." This shapes every word of the answer.

steps.title: Why does it work?
steps.body: AI has absorbed enormous amounts of writing from editors, coaches, planners, and experts. When you name a role, you tell it which kind of writing to draw from. The answer shifts immediately.

steps.title: Picture this
steps.body: You need to write a difficult email to a colleague. You type your message into AI and ask it to fix the tone. The answer is fine — but a bit generic. What if you told it to act as a professional communication coach first?

steps.title: Role prompt in action
steps.prompt: Act as a professional communication coach. I need to send a message to a colleague who missed an important deadline. Help me write something that is firm but keeps the relationship positive.

steps.title: The formula
steps.body: Role prompt formula: "Act as [role]. [Your request with context.]" The role comes first. The request comes second. Context — your specific details — makes it personal.

steps.title: Quick check
steps.question: What does a role prompt do?
options: It tells AI which style and perspective to use for its answer.
It lets AI access the internet.
feedback: Speed is not the point — quality is. A role shapes the style of the answer.
Exactly. The role tells AI which perspective and voice to use.
Role prompts are about style, not access to new information.
Length is not the goal — relevance and tone are.

steps.title: True or false?
steps.question: You can only use role prompts for professional work tasks.
feedback: Actually, role prompts work for anything — planning a trip, writing a toast, coaching yourself through a decision.
Right. Roles like "travel planner," "life coach," or "recipe expert" work just as well outside of work.

steps.title: Roles that work well
steps.body: Here are roles people use every day: editor (fix my writing), life coach (help me think through a decision), project planner (break this into steps), expert explainer (explain this simply), devil's advocate (poke holes in my plan).

steps.title: Match the role to the task

pairs.left: Rewrite my message — it sounds too harsh
Help me decide whether to take this new job
pairs.right: Act as an unbiased career coach

pairs.left: Explain compound interest like I am twelve

steps.title: Good fit?
steps.question: Act as a nutritionist. Help me plan simple, healthy lunches for a busy week — I do not cook much and I have about 15 minutes at noon.
feedback: Yes — giving AI a role plus your constraints makes this a great prompt.
Actually, this is a strong use of role prompting. Context plus role equals useful answer.

steps.title: Good fit?
steps.question: Act as my doctor and diagnose the pain in my chest.
feedback: Be careful here. AI can share general information, but chest pain needs a real doctor.
Right. For real medical concerns, see a professional. AI should not replace that.

steps.title: Fill in the blank
steps.question: To use a role prompt, start your message with the words "Act as a ______" before making your request.
feedback: Exactly — naming a role first shapes everything that follows.
Close. The key idea: name the role before you make your request.

steps.title: Build a role prompt

fields.label: What do you need help with?
fields.placeholder: e.g. decide whether to apply for a promotion

fields.label: What should AI know about your situation?
fields.placeholder: e.g. I have been in this role two years and feel ready but nervous

steps.template: Act as {{role}}. I need your help to {{task}}. Here is my situation: {{context}}.

steps.title: Copy your role prompt
steps.body: Copy the prompt you just built and paste it into your AI tool of choice — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Grok.

steps.title: Paste what you got back
steps.body: Paste the AI's response here. We will save it so you can review it later.

questions.label: Did the role prompt feel different from yesterday's plain question?
What role would you try next, and for what task?

questions.question: What is the best way to start a role prompt?
options: Act as [role]. Then make your request.

questions.question: Role prompts only work for business tasks.
A role prompt works because it tells AI which ______ and perspective to use.

steps.title: Day 2 complete
steps.body: You now know how to give AI a job before you ask your question. That single habit — naming a role — will make your prompts noticeably more useful from here on. See you tomorrow.
